item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny you ask. I know I had a specific reason for choosing that number, but it's been so long I don't remember it anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a scout for some kind of extra dimensional invasion, sent here to learn every possible way humans kill things so the invading soldiers can prepare. Every time they test this machine, they are making it harder to repel this eventual invasion. However, as long as we keep testing it regularly, the invasion won’t happen, not until they’re sure they’ve seen every possible means of being killed. This is why SCP-001-EX said testing should continue indefinitely.

Optionally, the invading species are the puppies. The machine can’t repeat termination methods because they adapt immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: anyone know whats up with the 368 cookies? that seems like something that'd be a blatant reference to real life. Is it referring to an event or is it just an in-universe mystery?

item.title: "Yeah I'm probably going to hell for liking this. I'll play again"
item.content:encoded:
on one hand, I want to hate this in the same way most people hate a character that "kicks the dog", but on the other, I'm aware enough to realize that's kind of the point (that I'm supposed to hate the machine not the article). This is disturbing, but well-written, and bonus points for including an experiment log (as much as that adds to "disturbing" point).
also, what BetaRayquaza (see previous comment) about this worsening my view of claw machines also applies, though in my case I was neutral before. It's just an arcade machine… until it isn't

item.title: Pure Evil
item.content:encoded: This thing is pure evil, but the "Yeah, you're totally going to hell for this. Play again?" at the end made it seem even eviler because it was blaming the dog murder on the player. It makes people kill a puppy in exchange for a cookie, diabolical. +1 for successfully making me despise claw machines even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, completely literally, laughed out loud. Thumbs up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article made me wheeze with laughter over a magic an anomalous claw game that kills puppies. I feel filthy, take this contaminated +1 and a cookie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

My headcanon is that SCP-1459 is a demon and the 'You're going to Hell for this' is completely true and literal. The demon realized humans were getting too smart for the classic deal with the devil to work anymore since everyone knows that trick now, so it changed tactics. You'd be surprised what shit people will do when it's framed as a 'joke' or 'game'. Also, the executive who gave the order to continue testing is Roger/O5-11. Though that does leave one to wonder why he got off so light in the afterlife compared to what he did if this interpretation is correct…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me a lot of those horrible flash games you used to find on Newgrounds, where you'd just beat up a weird doll or an animal or a politician or whatever over and over. I think what I really like about this article isn't the horror of how all these poor puppies are getting killed, and more how it reminds me of those flash games and how those games feed off of and encourage the player's sadistic and sociopathic tendencies, with the little self-aware "You're totally going to Hell for this" message reinforcing that.

I have a little headcanon that the research staff on this all start to have trouble with empathizing with people and become less sensitive to acts of cruelty, and nobody's really sure if that's an anomalous effect of the SCP or if that's just what happens when you work around something like this for too long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: '"Oh, you dog!" made me have to put my computer down for a couple minutes and just absolutely lose my shit. This is good. I like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is Awesome

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Firstly, this is referenced by SCP-001-EX, by the same author. A link that relates to that reference would work well. As such, I recommend changing this:

By executive order, testing is to continue indefinitely.

To:

By executive order, testing is to continue indefinitely.

Nextly, Sugarcomb Confections has been used by PeppersGhost in other articles, SCP-1921 and SCP-2471. 2471 already links back to this SCP, so I feel linking to 1921 would be more appropriate.

Changing this:

in partnership with Sugarcomb Confections.

To:

in partnership with Sugarcomb Confections.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Yeah, we are totally going to hell for this.
Hah, this SCP is hilarious. Good job. Has anyone yet found out what the initialism stands for?

item.title: Re: So close!
item.content:encoded:
Huh. After chewing on that angle for a couple hours… well, sorry if I'm just spelling out what everyone else got, but I think I finally get it.

If you'd used the ‘bad things will happen if you don't play the game’ idea, that would have invalidated the point of YWTGTHFT entirely, since playing the game would become a noble act. And why would the fine folks at The Foundation worry about going to Hell, anyways? They’re practically there already!

And that’s the thing about working for The Foundation. It’s like being a nurse, or a soldier. You can only laugh at the terrible shit going on around you, or succumb to depression and dismay. You need something to distract you from how insignificant you are in this massive melting-pot of crazy, and how you must operate as a cog in this incomprehensible machine if you want to do any good at all. You find humor wherever it can be scraped up.

So, morale is a perfectly good reason for this to have not been chucked in a closet. And it’s understandable that everyone silently agrees to not talk about how fucked-up this job has made them. Best not to dwell. Aside from the obvious humor aspect, this scip also lets the player feel a modicum of control over something that is, while supremely fucked, still ultimately harmless, if only for a while.

I appreciate this scip all the more now for the subtlety. And hey, maybe I could stand to un-jam the stick a couple inches; we're here to have fun, after all.

+1

item.title: Re: So close!
item.content:encoded: This is a valid complaint. However, for what it's worth, there is at least one in-universe reason—it just isn't something the Foundation would readily admit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course not. We must find these two companies and crosslink them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck Pepper? Like… just what the fuck?

+1

But still what's wrong with you?

item.title: Re: So close!
item.content:encoded: Wow, how did I manage to miss that three times?! Thanks for pointing that out, I feel more than a little silly now. After re-reading again and giving it some more thought, my opinion hasn’t changed too much but the critique’s been edited.

item.title: Re: So close!
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1459-1 instances may occasionally be subjected to anomalous changes to their physiology or behaviors in order to facilitate the method of destruction dictated by the player.

Felt justified to point out that it says this after the "innately non-anomalous" part, since I added Mutiny At Sea quite a while ago

Can't argue against the needlessly cruel aspect, since that's really what makes this article so appalling and morbidly humorous.

item.title: So close!
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like this scip; I don't think another one's made me laugh this hard since the Pan-Dimensional Vending Machine. But, two one major flaw keeps me from upvoting. Without a solid reason for continued testing of this scip, this (to me) falls squarely into lolFoundation territory. Secondly, SCP-1459-1 should not be classified as 'non-anomalous'. I've rescinded reason two due to an oversight on my part.

Basically, the whole thing seems needlessly cruel and wholly pointless. Which is what makes this scip so chock full of delicious dark humor, but, from an in-universe perspective, The Foundation has nothing to gain from continued experimentation. My suspension of disbelief just doesn't hold; by all means, the Foundation should've chucked this into a locked closet by now and called it a day.

Now, if there were a reason provided for continued testing, namely that the machine would do Something Bad if left idle long enough, than that would get rid of the problem entirely. Plus, since no death can be requested twice, the Foundation can't just assign someone to hit a button and repeat a phrase, so the testing logs would be totally unaffected.

If the author is against creating a reason for continued testing (which, hey, it's their scip and it's still well-written and hilarious, either way,) then I think this should be re-classified as a -J.

Until the above issue is resolved thru either an author edit or scip reclassification, I'm novoting. I love this scip and want to see it be the best it can be! (I'm still adding a death to and upvoting the Extended Testing Log, though. Shit's funny.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I just found John Wick's least favorite SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm… The more I think about this, the more I think that while it's well-written on a surface level, it… just doesn't make sense. There's no apparent reason to keep testing it: it won't teleport away to entrap unsuspecting civilians if left unused, for example, and there's no knowledge that can really be gained from it. Forget morality for a moment (even though the Ethics Committee is a thing): it's wasteful to do anything other than lock this in a cell and let it collect dust.

If this was a -J, it'd be freed from those sorts of considerations. As is, I gotta no-vote the main article and downvote the supplement (for making the inconsistency even more glaring).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Experiment #: 0051
Player: Dr. Yeatley
Statement: "Reality TV."
Result: A 1958 General Electric television set fell on SCP-1459-1. The set then powered on and replayed the event.

I realise that the use of it being "General Electric" is just so they can have a brand name, but some other brands were blacked out. I have a conspiracy about General Electric in this universe. And the coolest part of this site is that if it doesn't exist, I can make it exist.

Hwehwehweh

Anyways, you made an entire SCP about killing puppies. You are evil and terrible and I hate you.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could +∞, I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me really sad, but I can't give it a -1 for that. It was creative and well-written, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're a horrible person. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I died a little bit inside.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to meet the person who wrote this and have a nice conversation. Behind closed doors.With a stick. A big stick.

Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was honestly expecting the Batman test to say it was invalid or something, since Batman doesn't (usually) kill people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is brilliantly sadistic, which makes me want to show it to my girlfriend, but I get the feeling that if I do, I won't have a girlfriend anymore.

+1

item.title: Re: U ALL ARE CRUEL BASTARDS
item.content:encoded:
edwardzzzz9, even if something isn't real, that doesn't mean readers can't have an emotional reaction to what they read about.

There are some readers who don't upvote funny mainlist articles because they don't think mainlist articles should be funny. Similarly, this user doesn't know if they should neutral or downvote because of how cruel the researchers are as portrayed in the piece.

item.title: Re: U ALL ARE CRUEL BASTARDS
item.content:encoded:
It's not real, just like everything else on this site.

Also, I forgot to upvote this.

item.title: U ALL ARE CRUEL BASTARDS
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking whether to not vote at all or down vote.

Edit: stumbled across this again. Haven't decide whether to invite or downvote.
I think I saw someone mentioned that the researchers were trying to save the dogs… Well, I guess not all of them. Think the first one kinda enjoyed it. I thought Foundation is all about "cold, not cruel"?

Anyways, Zyn is right on why I am hesitating to vote. Like, I cried hard because Yondu died in GOTG 2, even though it isn't real. Emotion is real, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would read 200 of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it bad that I laughed hysterically while reading the logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did we use this thing more than once?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Badly written

Yes, I'm glad we're on the same page with that taste is subjective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How? It's a box that produces puppy dogs for a measly price that can be destroyed in hundreds and thousands of ways. Unavoidably. If that isn't one of a sadist's wet dreams, what could be?

I admit I didn't get the YWTGTHFT reference, but even then, it doesn't justify anything. If I went on a hyperbole - and said what if a group of people murdered stray dogs and then went "We're totally going to hell everybody" (see here at 38:15) - that doesn't change anything about the act.

Maybe we have read a whole another log (and I didn't read the extended one) but the only guy trying to save the dog is the "Please, no kill dog." That's it.

Again: I get why people might like it and I admit the log is full of goofy, but this is all I saw.

Believe me, I am not trying to pick an argument either, but here is my explanation. If you disagree, please do, but I have no need to convince you or be convinced. It's a matter of taste.

DISCLAIMER: I don't own a dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I thought wish fulfillments are frowned upon here, heavily.

No, plagiarism, trolling and miscellaneous asshattery is frowned upon. Badly written wish fulfillment is downvoted. Well-written wish fulfillment is upvoted. Not that this is in any way wish fulfillment, mind you. Both OZ Ouroboros and Sound Chaser are completely right about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the same boat as SoundChaser. This really doesn't feel like wish fulfillment at all, just a terrible machine meant to make you feel terrible for playing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Er… I can understand criticising this as tasteless, or too silly, but how on earth is this wish fulfilment? Surely that implies something good about either the thing itself or what it does for people? Half the people in the log feel guilty, several try to save the puppies, and the company who made the thing are literally called "Yeah, We're Totally Going To Hell For This". I mean, I… guess if you like watching dogs die whilst eating your least favourite type of biscuit, but that strikes me as extremely unlikely. Not trying to pick an argument or anything, I'm just genuinely curious as to your reasoning here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, eh. I mean, I get why is it anomalous and that the log is full of silly, but this just feels like a sadistic wish fulfillment. And I thought wish fulfillments are frowned upon here, heavily. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel terrible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is so horrible. Even more than 231.

Note: Many cookies were dispensed.

Fuck you for making me laugh. I hope you choke on your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: truely messed up, but I love it all the same +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate this I hate this I-

/upvotes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking hilarious. I had to attempt to explain to people why I was laughing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two things I don't understand at all:
1) How is Valera even allowed to do this without an upgrade to Level 2?

Level 1 security clearances are given to personnel working in proximity to but with no direct, indirect, or informational access to anomalous objects or entities in containment.

2) Why does she have to do this anyway? Can't they just contain this thing and forget about it? Does something bad happen if you don't use it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Technician Valera must be so done with all of this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. Valera is hilarious. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looking back on this article… I can't believe this article actually existed for me to look back on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Caucasian woman in a red evening gown emerged from SCP-1459's upper chamber, sat on SCP-1459-1's face, and began moaning in apparent pleasure. After continuing this activity for another 5 hours, both individuals were removed via SCP-1459's claw.

Oh god what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's intentionally over-the-top. You're supposed to feel like shit if you use this thing, and if you continue to use it out of morbid curiosity. That's the gimmick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, but this is way too needlessly cruel and violent for me. It doesn't feel like a creative SCP, just some gross snuff fantasy. While a couple of the experiments may have been neat ideas, there's not nearly enough creativity here to justify the torture porn. Downvoted without a moment's hesitation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of my favorite SCPs and I'm totally going to hell for laughing at this.

The exact variety dispensed to a given player is often the one that the player has the lowest preference for.

This is deliciously petty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was going to upvote the other day but couldn't log in. Great idea.

item.title: Re: here.
item.content:encoded: This is the discussion page for SCP-1459. If you'd like to discuss your own unrelated work, please do so in the Drafts and Critiques forum.

item.title: Re: here.
item.content:encoded:
I realize now that i have indeed overreacted, creepy pasta is actually a trigger for me.
Also i have submitted several drafts of my lego joke for feedback.
